title: Kotlin Syntax
content:
### Kotlin Syntax Basics

Kotlin syntax is clean and expressive. Semicolons are optional, and the language emphasizes readability.

---

### Package Declaration

```kotlin
package com.example.myapp
```

Package declarations go at the top of the file.

---

### Entry Point

The `main()` function is the entry point of every Kotlin application.

```kotlin
fun main() {
    println("Hello, World!")
}
```

---

### Comments

```kotlin
// Single-line comment

/* Multi-line
   comment */

/**
 * Documentation comment
 */
```

---

### Variables

| Keyword | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `val` | Immutable reference (read-only) |
| `var` | Mutable reference (read-write) |

```kotlin
val name = "Alice"  // Cannot be reassigned
var age = 25        // Can be reassigned
```

---

### String Templates

```kotlin
val name = "Bob"
println("Hello, $name!")         // Hello, Bob!
println("Length: ${name.length}") // Length: 3
```

> **Key Point:** Kotlin supports string templates with `$` and `${}` syntax.

---

### Type Inference

Kotlin infers types automatically when possible.

```kotlin
val message = "Hello"  // Inferred as String
val count = 42         // Inferred as Int
```

---

### Basic Types

| Type | Size | Example |
|------|------|---------|
| Byte | 8-bit | `val b: Byte = 127` |
| Short | 16-bit | `val s: Short = 32767` |
| Int | 32-bit | `val i: Int = 2147483647` |
| Long | 64-bit | `val l: Long = 9223372036854775807L` |
| Float | 32-bit | `val f: Float = 3.14F` |
| Double | 64-bit | `val d: Double = 3.14159` |
| Char | 16-bit | `val c: Char = 'A'` |
| Boolean | 1-bit | `val b: Boolean = true` |
| String | - | `val s: String = "Hello"` |

---

### If Expression

In Kotlin, `if` is an expression that returns a value.

```kotlin
val max = if (a > b) a else b
```

> **Key Point:** No ternary operator in Kotlin -- use `if` as an expression instead.
codeExample:
fun main() {
    val name = "Kotlin"
    val year = 2016
    println("$name was created in $year.")
    
    val message = if (year > 2010) "Modern language" else "Legacy language"
    println(message)
}

quiz.question: Which keyword declares an immutable variable in Kotlin?
Does Kotlin require semicolons at the end of statements?
